repo_prior_art.cite: pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 and pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 both use zero-budget/zero-bound recovery corners for sensitivity intervals.

published_prior_art.cite: XuZhaoDing2026FDID: factorial parallel trends gives the FDID causal-moderation point-identification corner.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1: shared-coordinate support aggregation; pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1: joint support body, but neither targets FDID causal moderation.

published_prior_art.cite: none; XuZhaoDing2026FDID has FDID point ID, RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends has DID sensitivity, and BalkePearl1997Bounds/Demuynck2015LPBounds provide generic finite-LP technology.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 states sign-loss frontiers as estimate/support ratios; panel_honest_did_phase_v1 states breakdown thresholds as |target|/support.

published_prior_art.cite: RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends: support-style sensitivity and breakdown logic, but not an FDID causal-moderation sign frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the proposed sharp primal-dual FDID interval is a constructive object, but §9 does not compute R(P), A_rho(P), b_rho(P), primal endpoints, and dual certificates on a worked FDID instance from §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison cites RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends, BalkePearl1997Bounds, and Demuynck2015LPBounds only at paper level; it needs section/equation/page anchors for the exact sensitivity and LP gaps.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The critical scalar rho^-(P) is exhibited using a declared feasible set S_1(P), not by deriving the support h_P(c;1) from the proposal's FDID matrices R(P), A_rho(P), r(P), and b_rho(P).

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The load-bearing finite-cell completion map is not specified: R(P), r(P), A_rho(P), b_rho(P), and the 'full FDID causal-moderation problem' are named but not mathematically defined.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption ray is only local near zero, but the theorem gives a global formula and iff statement for rho^-(P) without assuming the crossing lies inside that local ray or that homogeneity is global.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 3
soundness_flags.one_line: rho^-(P) is defined as the infimum budget at which inf Theta_I becomes negative, so the headline iff is largely a threshold-definition unfold unless replaced by an independently characterized observable frontier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the full FDID latent completion explicitly and instantiate R(P), r(P), A_rho(P), and b_rho(P) for a small rational 2x2 example; compute both primal endpoints and dual multipliers.

Add location-level anchors for the closest comparator claims: Xu-Zhao-Ding's factorial-parallel-trends theorem/definition, Rambachan-Roth's restriction class/support construction, and Balke-Pearl/Demuynck LP sharpness results.
Repair Theorem 3 by either assuming global ray homogeneity or restricting the statement to budgets below a named local radius with tau_em(P)/h_P(c;1) inside that radius.
To reach flagship tier, make Conjecture 2 a strict FDID-specific sharpness theorem with a nontrivial pooled-coordinate phenomenon that cannot be obtained by direct generic LP duality.

recommended_next_step: Redraft around a fully specified FDID completion map and a worked primal-dual exhibit; otherwise this remains a field-tier LP sensitivity specialization.
